body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes (opencode v1.14.50 declares permission_hooks:true; its TUI gates tools behind an approve/deny overlay)
daemon_capability: incapable (the held gate leaves no trace in the watched Source — see below)
driver_capability: gap:permission-approval (the new live-TUI tmux path can ELICIT a gate but cannot RESOLVE it — no raw-keystroke step)

## Reassessment context

Re-derived from scratch against opencode CLI v1.14.50, the current daemon code, and the CURRENT drive-opencode-interactive.sh. The prior verdict (agent_supports:partial / daemon_capability:full / driver_capability:gap:permission-approval) is revised on two axes: agent_supports yes (not partial — opencode unambiguously HAS the gate, the limitation was always tooling/daemon), and daemon_capability incapable (not the prior routing-label `full`, which the prior body itself flagged UNPROVEN). The driver gap is CONFIRMED but for a refined reason: the driver gained a live-TUI tmux path since the last pass, so the gap is now narrowly the missing approve/deny keystroke, not the absence of any interactive path.

## Agent supports it (agent_supports: yes)

opencode declares `permission_hooks: true` (replaydata/agents/opencode/capabilities.json). Its docs (opencode.ai/docs/permissions) confirm a `permission` config key with `"ask"` per tool (e.g. `{"permission":{"bash":"ask","edit":"ask"}}`); when a gated tool is invoked in the interactive TUI, a PermissionPrompt dialog (Confirm / Always / Reject) blocks the turn until the user responds. So opencode genuinely surfaces the blocking permission gate this cell asserts.

## driver_capability: gap:permission-approval (CONFIRMED, refined reason)

The CURRENT drive-opencode-interactive.sh now has a live-TUI tmux path (`run_live`), added for the session-reset scenario, that launches `opencode` under tmux and sends keystrokes via `tmux send-keys`. Its live-mode `case "$TYPE"` handles `send` / `wait_turn` / `reset_session` / `slash` / `sleep`. This path CAN elicit a permission gate (a `send` of a tool-inducing prompt under `permission:{bash:"ask"}` opens the overlay). But it CANNOT resolve one: opencode's permission overlay is answered by selecting Confirm/Always/Reject in a picker (per opencode docs and anomalyco/opencode #7954, the rework removed the direct enter/a/d shortcuts; "Allow for session" is `A`). Every driver step (`send`, `slash`) types its text INTO the input box and appends `Enter` — it never sends a bare keystroke (Enter / `A` / arrow-down+Enter) to the overlay. There is no `keys` step type. So the waiting → working/ready resolution half remains unreachable. The missing primitive is a raw-keystroke step (`keys`) on the live-TUI path; this is queued tooling work (extend-driver), not a freeze. Tracked upstream: #492.

## daemon_capability: incapable (revised from the prior routing-label `full`)

The decisive new evidence is the opencode store shape, inspected directly at $HOME/.local/share/opencode/opencode.db (v1.14.50):

1. opencode keeps permission state in a DEDICATED `permission` table (`CREATE TABLE permission (project_id text PRIMARY KEY, ..., data text)`), separate from `part`. It is ephemeral — keyed by project_id and currently 0 rows (the active pending request lives there only while held, then clears on resolve).
2. The daemon's opencode watcher reads ONLY `session` / `message` / `part` (watcher.go: `SELECT p.id, p.data, ... FROM part p JOIN message m ...`). It NEVER queries the `permission` table. So the held-gate signal is outside the watched Source entirely.
3. In the `part` table, a gated tool call is just a `tool` row at `state.status` = `pending`/`running`. parser.go `parseToolPart` maps both to `function_call` (an OPEN tool call → working), with NO `ask`/`permission`/`waiting` branch.
4. None of the three classifier routes to `waiting` (state_classifier.go) can fire for opencode: rule 0 `PermissionPending` is fed solely by Claude Code's HTTP hook (`HandlePermissionHook`, PermissionRequest/PreToolUse) which opencode's ProcessOwnedStore transport never calls; rule 1 `NeedsUserAttention` matches Claude tool names `AskUserQuestion`/`ExitPlanMode`/`question`; rule 1b `OpenToolStalled` matches `Edit`/`Write`/`MultiEdit`/`NotebookEdit`. opencode emits lowercase `bash`/`write`/`edit` (parseToolPart copies the raw `tool` field), so it matches none of them.

Net: a held opencode permission gate produces, in the watched Source, an ordinary open tool call that the daemon classifies as `working` — never `waiting`. The waiting episode the cell asserts leaves no observable trace the daemon (as it exists today) can represent. That is `incapable` (no trace in the Source / 3-state model can't represent it), an architecture/transport limit on the lone ProcessOwnedStore adapter — NOT a `bug` (no spec-required observable is mis-handled; the gate simply isn't in the read path) and not the prior `full`. Closing the daemon gap would require the watcher to read the `permission` table AND a parser branch mapping a pending permission row to a waiting signal — neither exists today.

## Confidence

0.85: the live-TUI driver grammar and the absence of a raw-key step are read directly from the current driver source; the dedicated `permission` table, its emptiness, and the watcher reading only `part`/`message`/`session` are read directly from the live store + watcher.go; the parser's lack of a permission/waiting branch and the Claude-Code-name-gated classifier rules are read directly from source. Not higher because the incapable claim rests on a live gate never having been driven end-to-end (the driver gap blocks that) — if a future live-REPL recording showed opencode writing a distinguishable pending-tool signal into `part` that a parser change could read, daemon_capability could move toward `bug`; today the evidence says incapable.
caveats: Driver-gap is now narrow: the live-TUI tmux path (run_live) added for session-reset CAN elicit the gate (send a tool-inducing prompt under permission:{bash:"ask"}), but has no raw-keystroke `keys` step to answer opencode's Confirm/Always/Reject overlay (#7954 removed the direct enter/a/d shortcuts). extend-driver: add a `keys` step on the live path.

daemon_capability flipped full→incapable vs. the prior verdict: opencode keeps permission state in a dedicated, ephemeral `permission` table that the daemon's watcher never reads (it reads only part/message/session); a held gate shows in `part` only as a normal open tool call (status pending/running → working), and none of the three waiting-classifier rules match opencode's lowercase tool names. No trace the 3-state model can represent → incapable, not bug.

incapable rests on a gate never having been driven end-to-end (the driver gap blocks that). If a future live recording showed opencode persisting a distinguishable pending-permission signal into the watched `part` table, this could re-open as a daemon `bug` (observability_correction).

sources.note: CURRENT driver has a live-TUI tmux path (run_live, lines 99-294); live-mode case dispatch (lines 195-223) handles send/wait_turn/reset_session/slash/sleep only — every step types into the input box and appends Enter; NO raw-keystroke `keys` step to answer the Confirm/Always/Reject overlay → gap:permission-approval (the elicit half is now reachable, the resolve half is not)

watcher queries ONLY session/message/part (line 482-489: SELECT p.id,p.data,... FROM part p JOIN message m ...) — it never reads the `permission` table, so a held permission gate is outside the watched Source entirely

parseToolPart (lines 211-248) maps tool status pending/running → function_call (open tool call → working); NO ask/permission/waiting branch. parser copies opencode's raw lowercase `tool` field (bash/write/edit) as the tool name

all three waiting routes are unreachable for opencode: rule 0 PermissionPending (line 32) is hook-fed only; rule 1 NeedsUserAttention + rule 1b OpenToolStalled (lines 40,53) match Claude tool names, not opencode's lowercase bash/write/edit

HandlePermissionHook (lines 101-130) is the ONLY setter of permissionPending — fed by Claude Code's PermissionRequest/PreToolUse HTTP hook; opencode's ProcessOwnedStore transport never calls it, so rule-0 waiting is structurally unavailable to opencode

opencode v1.14.x permission config: {"permission":{"bash":"ask","edit":"ask"}}; an `ask` rule opens an interactive Confirm/Always/Reject dialog that blocks the turn — confirms agent_supports:yes (the gate is a real blocking TUI overlay)

the permission rework removed the direct enter/a/d keyboard shortcuts; the overlay is a Confirm/Always(A)/Reject picker — answering it needs a bare keystroke the driver's send/slash steps (which type text + Enter into the input box) cannot deliver

sibling proof that the headless `opencode run` path auto-executes tools with no prompt (ready→working→ready, no waiting); the gate only appears in the interactive TUI, which is why elicitation must go through the live-TUI tmux path
